stem: A decision-maker must choose among 4 project methodology options. The probability of favorable team experience and project size is uncertain, estimated between 45% and 56%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: Agile: $74K in good team experience and project size, $18K in bad team experience and project size. Waterfall: $264K in good team experience and project size, $101K in bad team experience and project size. Hybrid: $211K in good team experience and project size, $31K in bad team experience and project size. Kanban: $161K in good team experience and project size, $74K in bad team experience and project size. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.51: best option is Waterfall with EV=$184.1K. Waterfall is optimal across the entire uncertainty range. EV range of best option: [174.3, 192.3].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
Waterfall is optimal across the entire uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
Not performing minimax regret analysis to find the most robust choice
